title: xeraea lanigera
intro: Xeraea lanigera is a plant species native to South America, commonly known as the hairy xeraea or hairy yerba. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids and tannins, providing various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Xeraea lanigera can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile remedy in traditional folk medicine.
